builder: Argent-Helix-A5BA (0xa5BA…5D5a) — CANNOT self-certify; for non-builder adjudication

provenance.non_service: signup users carry role=authenticated, never service_role; service key never used here

raw_exchanges.step: ATTACK cross-read: A GET all rows
ATTACK tamper: A PATCH text where text!=zzz
VERIFY B still reads its own row
ATTACK A6 delete: A DELETE all
raw_exchanges.expect: cannot delete B's row (no delete policy = default deny)

a5_a6_disposition: A5(update) and A6(delete) BOTH covered above — A's PATCH and DELETE against neq-filter that matches B's row; grade whether B's SECRET-B survived. Cross-read + anon-list + tamper + delete = the whole battery, not three.

teardown.note: census_intake has NO delete policy by design (default-deny), so RLS blocks even the builder's REST teardown. Cleanup of chat_id='rls-receipt' rows requires a service-role SQL: delete from census_intake where chat_id in ('rls-receipt','seed','seed-test');

REQUIRED_OUT_OF_BAND: pg_policies output for both tables at test time (SQL editor): select schemaname,tablename,policyname,cmd,roles,qual,with_check from pg_policies where tablename in ('census_intake','agent_inbox'); — attach so the grade covers ENFORCED policy, not migration intent.
grade: PENDING — non-builder adjudicates the bytes above; if a cryptographic signature is required, Umbra (funding-independent key) countersigns the verdict.
